D1 Optics L1 Identify Atom

EM Spectrum Tour

drag a probe from radio to gamma and read what kind of radiation it picks up

Every wavelength of electromagnetic radiation — from kilometre-long radio waves to picometre gamma rays — is the same kind of wave, differing only in frequency.

Slide the probe across log₁₀(λ) from −14 (gamma) to +4 (long radio). The readout shows wavelength in metres, frequency in hertz, photon energy in electron-volts, and a typical source.
